## Verified codebase reference points (used throughout)

These are the load-bearing grep results every per-chapter doc cites back to.

| Topic | Finding | Evidence (`file:line`) |
|---|---|---|
| Production model wiring | Five `.pt` files: `student_teacher.pt`, `standing_sitting.pt`, `right_left.pt`, `forward_backward.pt`, `up_down.pt` | `backend/config/settings/base.py:280-284` |
| Triton model registry | 7 task keys: `posture_detection`, `gaze_horizontal`, `gaze_vertical`, `gaze_depth`, `behavior_all`, `person_detection`, `pose_estimation` | `backend/apps/pipeline/services/model_route_service.py:27-55` |
| Default behavior model list at runtime | `['person_detection', 'sitting_standing', 'attention_tracking', 'hand_raising', 'pose_estimation']` | `backend/apps/video_analysis/tasks.py:3234`, `:5304` |
| Repo title | `Exam Monitoring Dashboard` (no "Sentinel AI" branding anywhere in code) | `README.md:1` |
| Live-streaming transports | `GO2RTC_API_URL`, `GO2RTC_WHEP_URL`, `MEDIAMTX_WHEP_URL` + RTSP probe + URL resolution | `backend/config/settings/base.py:220-223`; `backend/apps/cameras/services.py:16-22`; `backend/apps/cameras/views.py:528,561,590` |
| YOLO26 | Exists ONLY as a planned integration for **pose** estimation. No YOLO26 binary-pair classifier exists in code. | `docs/yolo26_pose.md:3-12` |
| YOLO architectures wired into runtime | Only **YOLOv8-style decode** appears in production paths | `backend/apps/video_analysis/tasks.py:1571,1706` (`# Decode YOLOv8-style output tensor`); grep `yolov9|yolov10|yolov11|yolov12` across `backend/**/*.py` = 0 hits |
| Audio / facial recognition / LMS | **None implemented.** grep `audio|microphone|speech|voice|whisper` and `face_recognition|facial_recognition|face_embedding` and `Moodle|Open edX|LMS` in `backend/**/*.py` all return 0 hits | grep evidence |

## Cross-document consistency issues

| Issue | Ch 1 | Ch 2 | Ch 5 | Ch 7 | Reality |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Number of behavior classes | "ten distinct classes" | "eight behavior classes" | "five binary pairs" → 10 labels across 5 models | (not specified) | 5 deployed binary classifiers + pose + person-detector (`base.py:280-284`) |
| Product name | "intelligent surveillance framework" | "AI-Based Surveillance System for Detecting and Monitoring Students' Behaviours" | "the proposed system" | **"Sentinel AI"** | **"Exam Monitoring Dashboard"** (`README.md:1`) |
| YOLO architectures | "YOLOv8, v9, v10, v11, v12, YOLO-NAS" | various per study | "YOLOv8m, v8l, v9m, v9c, v10m, v10l, v11m, v11l, v12m, v12l, YOLO26" | (not specified) | Only **YOLOv8-style decode** wired (`tasks.py:1571`); YOLO26 = pose plan only |

## Recommended fixes (helpers)

A condensed action list for the thesis author:

1. **Chapter 1** — reconcile the class taxonomy with Chapter 5 (5 binary pairs, not 10 classes) and drop the YOLOv9/v10/v11/v12/YOLO-NAS list down to what was actually deployed. Add the missing engineering layer (Triton, BSIL, streaming bridge).
2. **Chapter 7** — pick ONE product name (`Sentinel AI` *or* `Exam Monitoring Dashboard` *or* `ApexGuard` — Chapter 5's filename hints at "ApexGuard") and remove the four false capability claims: facial recognition, audio monitoring, Moodle/Open edX integration, edge-compute hardware. None of these exist in code.
3. **Chapter 5** — clarify that YOLO26 is only being evaluated for **pose**, not as a 6th binary-pair classifier; either add an EDUNET dataset card with actual repository file paths or remove the named-dataset attribution. Add a paragraph on the Triton ensemble wrapper layer (it's the deployed inference path).
4. **Chapter 2** — fix reference #6 typo `SCP-Dataset` → `SCB-Dataset` (body text uses SCB; arXiv link `2304.02488` is SCB); fix reference #1 title pluralisation `the Student` → `Students`; add author attribution `Sumaiya et al.` to reference #2; fix reference #5 title `Examination` → `Examinations`.

## How to regenerate the PDF

```bash
python E:/grad_project/scripts/build_audit_pdf.py
# -> writes audit_report.pdf at the repo root
```

The script mirrors every fact in the per-chapter markdown files below. If a
markdown file is edited, update `build_audit_pdf.py` to match (the script is
the rendering layer; the markdown is the canonical record).
